agents.result:
### SUMMARY
Implemented customizable quick action buttons above the chat input. Buttons are loaded from the `quick_actions` setting (JSON array) with cursor-aware insertion via `selectionStart`/`selectionEnd`. A textarea editor was added to the Appearance settings panel where users can customize buttons (one per line). i18n keys were added for the settings UI labels. Build could not be verified via shell due to sub-agent restrictions, but JSON validation passed and the diff is clean.

### EVIDENCE
- `src/ui/chat/ChatInput.tsx:1` — `useEffect` added to imports; `getSetting` imported from `settingsRepo`
- `src/ui/chat/ChatInput.tsx:10-16` — `DEFAULT_QUICK_ACTIONS` constant with 5 default buttons
- `src/ui/chat/ChatInput.tsx:62-77` — `quickActions` state and `useEffect` to load from settings on mount
- `src/ui/chat/ChatInput.tsx:153-186` — button row JSX with cursor-aware insertion using `textareaRef.current.selectionStart`/`selectionEnd`, `requestAnimationFrame` for focus restore
- `src/ui/settings/AppearancePanel.tsx:1-2` — `useEffect`/`useState` and `getSetting`/`setSetting` imports
- `src/ui/settings/AppearancePanel.tsx:8-13` — `DEFAULT_QUICK_ACTIONS` constant (same defaults)
- `src/ui/settings/AppearancePanel.tsx:19-50` — load/save logic: reads JSON array from settings, joins/splits by newline for editing
- `src/ui/settings/AppearancePanel.tsx:131-165` — textarea + save button UI in AppearancePanel
- `src/i18n/cs.json:437-438` — `quickActionsTitle`/`quickActionsHint` Czech strings
- `src/i18n/en.json:433-434` — `quickActionsTitle`/`quickActionsHint` English strings
- `validate_data` on both JSON files returned `valid: true`

### CHANGES
- `src/ui/chat/ChatInput.tsx` — added quick action button row with cursor-aware text insertion, loaded from `getSetting("quick_actions")`
- `src/ui/settings/AppearancePanel.tsx` — added quick actions textarea editor with save functionality
- `src/i18n/cs.json` — added `settings.appearance.quickActionsTitle` and `quickActionsHint` Czech strings
- `src/i18n/en.json` — added `settings.appearance.quickActionsTitle` and `quickActionsHint` English strings

### RISKS
- The cursor insertion uses `requestAnimationFrame` to defer `focus()` + `setSelectionRange` after React state update; on some React versions or with concurrent mode this could race if the DOM hasn't updated yet — a `setTimeout(..., 0)` or `flushSync` would be more robust but is heavier.
- The `key={label}` prop on buttons will break if two buttons share the same label text; a synthetic key or index would be safer but the use case makes duplicate labels unlikely.
- Build was not verified via `tsc --noEmit` because shell access is restricted in this sub-agent — only JSON validation was run.

### BLOCKERS
None.

content.text:
### SUMMARY
Implemented customizable quick action buttons above the chat input. Buttons are loaded from the `quick_actions` setting (JSON array) with cursor-aware insertion via `selectionStart`/`selectionEnd`. A textarea editor was added to the Appearance settings panel where users can customize buttons (one per line). i18n keys were added for the settings UI labels. Build could not be verified via shell due to sub-agent restrictions, but JSON validation passed and the diff is clean.

### EVIDENCE
- `src/ui/chat/ChatInput.tsx:1` — `useEffect` added to imports; `getSetting` imported from `settingsRepo`
- `src/ui/chat/ChatInput.tsx:10-16` — `DEFAULT_QUICK_ACTIONS` constant with 5 default buttons
- `src/ui/chat/ChatInput.tsx:62-77` — `quickActions` state and `useEffect` to load from settings on mount
- `src/ui/chat/ChatInput.tsx:153-186` — button row JSX with cursor-aware insertion using `textareaRef.current.selectionStart`/`selectionEnd`, `requestAnimationFrame` for focus restore
- `src/ui/settings/AppearancePanel.tsx:1-2` — `useEffect`/`useState` and `getSetting`/`setSetting` imports
- `src/ui/settings/AppearancePanel.tsx:8-13` — `DEFAULT_QUICK_ACTIONS` constant (same defaults)
- `src/ui/settings/AppearancePanel.tsx:19-50` — load/save logic: reads JSON array from settings, joins/splits by newline for editing
- `src/ui/settings/AppearancePanel.tsx:131-165` — textarea + save button UI in AppearancePanel
- `src/i18n/cs.json:437-438` — `quickActionsTitle`/`quickActionsHint` Czech strings
- `src/i18n/en.json:433-434` — `quickActionsTitle`/`quickActionsHint` English strings
- `validate_data` on both JSON files returned `valid: true`

### CHANGES
- `src/ui/chat/ChatInput.tsx` — added quick action button row with cursor-aware text insertion, loaded from `getSetting("quick_actions")`
- `src/ui/settings/AppearancePanel.tsx` — added quick actions textarea editor with save functionality
- `src/i18n/cs.json` — added `settings.appearance.quickActionsTitle` and `quickActionsHint` Czech strings
- `src/i18n/en.json` — added `settings.appearance.quickActionsTitle` and `quickActionsHint` English strings

### RISKS
- The cursor insertion uses `requestAnimationFrame` to defer `focus()` + `setSelectionRange` after React state update; on some React versions or with concurrent mode this could race if the DOM hasn't updated yet — a `setTimeout(..., 0)` or `flushSync` would be more robust but is heavier.
- The `key={label}` prop on buttons will break if two buttons share the same label text; a synthetic key or index would be safer but the use case makes duplicate labels unlikely.
- Build was not verified via `tsc --noEmit` because shell access is restricted in this sub-agent — only JSON validation was run.

### BLOCKERS
None.

agents.prompt:
## Task: M20 — Conditions / Buffs / Debuffs System

Add a condition tracking system to personas. Conditions are temporary effects that modify gameplay: "Otrávený (3 dny, -2 Alchymie)", "Požehnaný (1 den, +1 Boj)", "Zlomená noha (7 dní, -50% rychlost)".

### Implementation:

**1. Persona field** — extend the Persona type with `conditions` (already JSON in DB):
```typescript
interface Condition {
  name: string;
  description: string; // what it does
  expiresAt: string | null; // ISO date-time or null = permanent
  modifiers?: { stat: string; value: number }[]; // optional numeric effects
}
```

**2. Already exists:** personas have JSON fields (`skills`, `inventory`) — add `conditions` similarly via migration 13.

**3. AI manages conditions via tags:**
- `[COND:+jméno:popis]` — adds condition
- `[COND:-jméno]` — removes condition
- `[COND:+jméno:popis:7d]` — adds condition expiring in 7 days

**4. Tag parsing** — extend `src/chat/inventoryTags.ts`:
- Parse `[COND:...]` tags
- Export `ConditionMutation` type

**5. Processing** — extend `src/chat/inventoryProcessor.ts`:
- Apply condition mutations to persona

**6. Prompt instruction** — in `src/prompt/promptBuilder.ts`:
- Add condition tag instructions to game tags section
- Only show when persona has progression !== "none"

**7. DB migration (13):**
```sql
ALTER TABLE personas ADD COLUMN conditions TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]';
```

**8. UI** — add conditions display to `PersonaForm.tsx`:
- Read-only list of active conditions
- Show: name, description, expiry (if any)
- Red for negative, green for positive, yellow for neutral

### Key files:
- `src-tauri/src/migrations.rs` — add migration 13
- `src/db/repositories/personasRepo.ts` — add conditions to types, add CRUD helpers
- `src/chat/inventoryTags.ts` — parse `[COND:...]` tags
- `src/chat/inventoryProcessor.ts` — process condition mutations
- `src/prompt/promptBuilder.ts` — condition tag instructions
- `src/ui/personas/PersonaForm.tsx` — show conditions list

OUTPUT: Summary, files changed.

agents.result:
### SUMMARY
Implemented the M20 Conditions / Buffs / Debuffs system across all 8 required files. Added `conditions` JSON column via migration 13, extended the Persona type with `ConditionEntry`, added `[COND:...]` tag parsing with duration support, wired condition mutations into the game response processor, added condition tag instructions plus persona rendering to the prompt builder, and added a read-only conditions display to PersonaForm with color-coded badges (red/green/yellow). Fixed a critical data-loss bug where PersonaForm saving would wipe conditions by not including them in the draft.

### EVIDENCE
- `src-tauri/src/migrations.rs:74-79` — migration 13 added to `all_migrations()` vector
- `src-tauri/src/migrations.rs:321-325` — `MIGRATION_013` constant with `ALTER TABLE personas ADD COLUMN conditions`
- `src/db/repositories/personasRepo.ts:15-20` — `ConditionEntry` interface matching task spec
- `src/db/repositories/personasRepo.ts:37` — `conditions` field added to `Persona` interface
- `src/db/repositories/personasRepo.ts:53` — `conditions?: ConditionEntry[]` added to `PersonaDraft`
- `src/db/repositories/personasRepo.ts:72` — `conditions: string` added to `PersonaRow`
- `src/db/repositories/personasRepo.ts:101` — `toPersona` parses `conditions` JSON
- `src/db/repositories/personasRepo.ts:167-176` — `createPersona` inserts conditions; `updatePersona` on line 198 includes conditions
- `src/db/repositories/personasRepo.ts:258-270` — `updatePersonaConditions` helper
- `src/chat/inventoryTags.ts:47-53` — `ConditionMutation` interface with `op`, `name`, `description`, `duration`
- `src/chat/inventoryTags.ts:57-65` — `parseDuration` helper for `7d`/`3h`/`1m` suffixes
- `src/chat/inventoryTags.ts:127-141` — regex `[COND:...]` parser with three variants
- `src/chat/inventoryProcessor.ts:69-95` — condition mutation application loop
- `src/prompt/promptBuilder.ts:57` — `conditions` added to `PersonaLike`
- `src/prompt/promptBuilder.ts:270-278` — conditions rendered in persona system core block
- `src/prompt/promptBuilder.ts:532-536` — condition tag instructions in game tags section
- `src/ui/personas/PersonaForm.tsx:356-397` — conditions display with color-coded badges
- `src/ui/personas/PersonaForm.tsx:49` — `conditions` preserved in `buildDraft()` to prevent data loss on save
- `src/i18n/cs.json:200-203` — Czech translations for conditions UI
- `src/i18n/en.json:197-200` — English translations for conditions UI
- `validate_data` on both `cs.json` and `en.json` — both valid JSON

### CHANGES
- `src-tauri/src/migrations.rs` — added migration 13 (ALTER TABLE personas ADD COLUMN conditions)
- `src/db/repositories/personasRepo.ts` — added ConditionEntry, conditions field, row mapping, CRUD wiring, updatePersonaConditions helper
- `src/chat/inventoryTags.ts` — added ConditionMutation type, parseDuration helper, [COND:...] regex parsing
- `src/chat/inventoryProcessor.ts` — applies condition mutations (add/remove/update) with expiry calculation
- `src/prompt/promptBuilder.ts` — added conditions to PersonaLike, rendered in system core and game tag instructions
- `src/ui/personas/PersonaForm.tsx` — read-only conditions display with color badges; preserved conditions in buildDraft
- `src/i18n/cs.json` — added `form.fields.conditions` and `form.conditionsPermanent` Czech strings
- `src/i18n/en.json` — added `form.fields.conditions` and `form.conditionsPermanent` English strings

### RISKS
- **Race condition on persona writes**: `inventoryProcessor` reads persona → mutates → writes full row, but another concurrent operation could overwrite. Same pattern as existing inventory/skills processing; mitigation would be optimistic locking or per-field writes.
- **Regex greediness**: The `[COND:...]` regex uses `[^:\]]*` for description which won't match colons inside descriptions. Descriptions containing `:` would be truncated. Mitigation: use a more permissive parser if needed.
- **Unused import removed**: `updatePersonaConditions` helper exists but isn't called in the current flow; kept for external callers (e.g., direct API calls). Not a risk but worth noting.
- **No automated tests added**: No test file existed for `inventoryTags.ts` or `inventoryProcessor.ts`. Manual verification needed.

### BLOCKERS
None.

   420│       const mmr = lambda * rel - (1 - lambda) * maxSim;

   427│     const [next] = remaining.splice(bestIdx, 1);
   428│     selected.push(next);
   429│   }

   431│   return selected;

content.thinking:
I notice the migration version jumps from 11 to 13. The task says "migration 13". Let me check if there was supposed to be a migration 12. Looking at the task: "**7. DB migration (13):**". There's no migration 12. That's fine — the task explicitly says migration 13. Perhaps migration 12 was done elsewhere or is reserved.

Now let me verify all files are consistent. Let me check:
1. The `ConditionEntry` type has `modifiers?: { stat: string; value: number }[]` — but in `PersonaForm.tsx`, the condition rendering accesses `c.modifiers` which might be `undefined` for some conditions. That's handled correctly with `c.modifiers && c.modifiers.length > 0`.

2. In `promptBuilder.ts`, the `PersonaLike` interface now has `conditions` but the type uses `Array<{ ... modifiers?: Array<...> }>` which is equivalent to the repo type. Good.

3. The `buildSystemCore` function now renders conditions in the persona block. And the game tags section shows condition instructions. Good.

4. The `inventoryTags.ts` regex for `[COND:...]` — let me verify the regex:
```
/\[COND:([+-])([^:\]]+)(?::([^:\]]*))?(?::(\d+[dhm]))?\]/gi
```
This matches:
- `[COND:+name]` — minimal add (no desc, no duration) — should still work since both optional groups can be missing
- `[COND:+name:description]` — add with desc
- `[COND:+name:description:7d]` — add with desc and duration
- `[COND:-name]` — remove

The regex uses `[^:\]]+` for the name (one or more non-colon, non-bracket chars) and `[^:\]]*` for the description (zero or more). This handles empty descriptions fine.

Let me also double-check the `parseDuration` function handles all cases. It returns null for invalid inputs, and `parseDuration(c.duration) ?? 0` in the processor handles null by falling back to 0ms (which means the condition expires immediately). Wait — that's a problem!

Actually, I use `parseDuration(c.duration) ?? 0` — if the duration is null (no duration), `parseDuration(null)` would be called, but `null` doesn't match the regex `/^(\d+)([dhm])$/i`, so it returns null, and then `?? 0` makes it 0, meaning the condition would expire at `Date.now() + 0` = immediately.

Wait, let me re-check. The duration in the tag is like `7d` or `3h`. If `c.duration` is `null` (when no duration is specified in the tag), then `parseDuration(null)` — but null doesn't match the regex, so it returns null, and `?? 0` makes it 0. That would make the `expiresAt` be `now`. But actually, `null` means permanent — so I should NOT call `parseDuration` when `c.duration` is null.

Let me re-check the processor code:
```
const expiresAt = c.duration
  ? new Date(Date.now() + (parseDuration(c.duration) ?? 0)).toISOString()
  : null;
```

Yes, the ternary checks `c.duration` first. So if `c.duration` is null/falsy, `expiresAt` is `null` (permanent). If `c.duration` is a string like `"7d"`, `parseDuration("7d")` returns `7*24*60*60*1000`, and `expiresAt` is set correctly. If `c.duration` is an invalid string, `parseDuration` returns `null`, `?? 0` makes it 0, and `expiresAt` is `now`. That's still a fallback but acceptable — an invalid duration makes the condition expire immediately.

This all looks correct.

Let me also verify the `PersonaForm.tsx` conditions section — the `conditionsPermanent` translation key is accessed as `t("form.conditionsPermanent")`. With the `useTranslation(["personas", "common"])`, this resolves to `personas.form.conditionsPermanent` — which is correctly placed in the JSON.

content.thinking:
The `personasStore.update` calls `updatePersona(id, patch)` where `patch` is a `PersonaUpdate`. The `PersonaUpdate` is `Omit<PersonaDraft, "avatarPath">`, which now includes `conditions?: ConditionEntry[]`. 

However, the `PersonaForm` builds its draft without including `conditions`:

```typescript
const buildDraft = (): PersonaDraft | PersonaUpdate => ({
    name,
    gender,
    age: age ? parseInt(age, 10) : null,
    race,
    appearance,
    progression,
    skills: skills.filter((s) => s.name.trim()),
    inventory: inventory.filter((inv) => inv.item.trim()),
});
```

It doesn't include `conditions`! This means that when the user saves the form, `conditions` would be `undefined`, and the SQL UPDATE would set `conditions = JSON.stringify(undefined ?? [])` → `"[]"`. This would WIPE OUT any existing conditions when the user edits the persona form!

This is a serious bug. I need to fix the `PersonaForm` to preserve existing conditions when saving. Let me update the `buildDraft` function to include conditions.

content.text: Critical bug: `PersonaForm.buildDraft()` doesn't include `conditions`, which means saving the form would wipe all conditions. Let me fix this:

    appearance,

content.text:
### SUMMARY
Implemented the M20 Conditions / Buffs / Debuffs system across all 8 required files. Added `conditions` JSON column via migration 13, extended the Persona type with `ConditionEntry`, added `[COND:...]` tag parsing with duration support, wired condition mutations into the game response processor, added condition tag instructions plus persona rendering to the prompt builder, and added a read-only conditions display to PersonaForm with color-coded badges (red/green/yellow). Fixed a critical data-loss bug where PersonaForm saving would wipe conditions by not including them in the draft.

### EVIDENCE
- `src-tauri/src/migrations.rs:74-79` — migration 13 added to `all_migrations()` vector
- `src-tauri/src/migrations.rs:321-325` — `MIGRATION_013` constant with `ALTER TABLE personas ADD COLUMN conditions`
- `src/db/repositories/personasRepo.ts:15-20` — `ConditionEntry` interface matching task spec
- `src/db/repositories/personasRepo.ts:37` — `conditions` field added to `Persona` interface
- `src/db/repositories/personasRepo.ts:53` — `conditions?: ConditionEntry[]` added to `PersonaDraft`
- `src/db/repositories/personasRepo.ts:72` — `conditions: string` added to `PersonaRow`
- `src/db/repositories/personasRepo.ts:101` — `toPersona` parses `conditions` JSON
- `src/db/repositories/personasRepo.ts:167-176` — `createPersona` inserts conditions; `updatePersona` on line 198 includes conditions
- `src/db/repositories/personasRepo.ts:258-270` — `updatePersonaConditions` helper
- `src/chat/inventoryTags.ts:47-53` — `ConditionMutation` interface with `op`, `name`, `description`, `duration`
- `src/chat/inventoryTags.ts:57-65` — `parseDuration` helper for `7d`/`3h`/`1m` suffixes
- `src/chat/inventoryTags.ts:127-141` — regex `[COND:...]` parser with three variants
- `src/chat/inventoryProcessor.ts:69-95` — condition mutation application loop
- `src/prompt/promptBuilder.ts:57` — `conditions` added to `PersonaLike`
- `src/prompt/promptBuilder.ts:270-278` — conditions rendered in persona system core block
- `src/prompt/promptBuilder.ts:532-536` — condition tag instructions in game tags section
- `src/ui/personas/PersonaForm.tsx:356-397` — conditions display with color-coded badges
- `src/ui/personas/PersonaForm.tsx:49` — `conditions` preserved in `buildDraft()` to prevent data loss on save
- `src/i18n/cs.json:200-203` — Czech translations for conditions UI
- `src/i18n/en.json:197-200` — English translations for conditions UI
- `validate_data` on both `cs.json` and `en.json` — both valid JSON

### CHANGES
- `src-tauri/src/migrations.rs` — added migration 13 (ALTER TABLE personas ADD COLUMN conditions)
- `src/db/repositories/personasRepo.ts` — added ConditionEntry, conditions field, row mapping, CRUD wiring, updatePersonaConditions helper
- `src/chat/inventoryTags.ts` — added ConditionMutation type, parseDuration helper, [COND:...] regex parsing
- `src/chat/inventoryProcessor.ts` — applies condition mutations (add/remove/update) with expiry calculation
- `src/prompt/promptBuilder.ts` — added conditions to PersonaLike, rendered in system core and game tag instructions
- `src/ui/personas/PersonaForm.tsx` — read-only conditions display with color badges; preserved conditions in buildDraft
- `src/i18n/cs.json` — added `form.fields.conditions` and `form.conditionsPermanent` Czech strings
- `src/i18n/en.json` — added `form.fields.conditions` and `form.conditionsPermanent` English strings

### RISKS
- **Race condition on persona writes**: `inventoryProcessor` reads persona → mutates → writes full row, but another concurrent operation could overwrite. Same pattern as existing inventory/skills processing; mitigation would be optimistic locking or per-field writes.
- **Regex greediness**: The `[COND:...]` regex uses `[^:\]]*` for description which won't match colons inside descriptions. Descriptions containing `:` would be truncated. Mitigation: use a more permissive parser if needed.
- **Unused import removed**: `updatePersonaConditions` helper exists but isn't called in the current flow; kept for external callers (e.g., direct API calls). Not a risk but worth noting.
- **No automated tests added**: No test file existed for `inventoryTags.ts` or `inventoryProcessor.ts`. Manual verification needed.

### BLOCKERS
None.

## Task: M23 — Calendar / Seasons / Time System

We already have `gameTime.ts` and `gameTime.test.ts` for tracking in-game time. Now add a calendar system with named days, months, seasons, and special dates.

### Implementation:

**1. Calendar data** — `src/memory/calendar.ts` (new file):
```typescript
export interface CalendarDate {
  year: number; dayOfYear: number; month: string; day: number; season: string;
}
export function formatCalendarDate(date: CalendarDate): string // "15. Jarního větru, Rok 847"
export function advanceDay(current: CalendarDate): CalendarDate
export function getSeason(dayOfYear: number): string
```

Define 12 months + 4 seasons. Czech names for fantasy feel: "Jarní vítr", "Měsíc probuzení", etc.

**2. Settings** — in `src/db/repositories/settingsRepo.ts`:
- Store calendar as JSON: `{ year: 847, dayOfYear: 45 }` in setting `"game_calendar"`
- Initialize on first chat creation from `gameTime.ts` state

**3. Prompt instruction** — in `src/prompt/promptBuilder.ts`:
- Add current date/season to the system prompt: `[DNEŠNÍ DATUM] 15. Jarního větru, Rok 847 (Jaro)`
- AI can reference weather, seasonal events, NPC schedules
- Tag: `[TIME:+1d]` advances time by 1 day (also triggers sunset/dawn effects)

**4. UI** — show current date in ChatScreen header (small, next to character name):
- Format: "📅 15. Jarního větru, 847"
- Click to expand with season info

**5. Season effects** (prompt only, no mechanics):
- Spring: "Příroda se probouzí. Cesty jsou blátivé. Bylinky raší."
- Summer: "Horké dny. Obchodníci jsou na cestách. Bouřky jsou časté."
- Autumn: "Sklizeň. Listí padá. Lovná zvěř je aktivní."
- Winter: "Mráz. Sníh. Cesty jsou nebezpečné. Noci dlouhé."

### Key files:

- MODIFY: `src/db/repositories/settingsRepo.ts`

All changes look correct. Let me now produce the final report.

### SUMMARY

Implemented M23 Calendar / Seasons / Time System: created `src/memory/calendar.ts` with a 360-day fantasy calendar (12 Czech-named months, 4 seasons, genitive forms for date formatting), added calendar storage helpers to `src/db/repositories/settingsRepo.ts`, integrated `[DNEŠNÍ DATUM]` prompt block + `[TIME:+1d]` tag instruction into `src/prompt/promptBuilder.ts`, wired calendar initialization and description into `src/stores/chatStore.ts`, added `advanceAndPersistCalendar`/`ensureCalendarInitialized` to `src/memory/memoryEngine.ts`, and rendered the current date with click-to-expand season info in `ChatScreen.tsx` header. Also wrote comprehensive unit tests in `src/memory/calendar.test.ts`.

### EVIDENCE

- `src/memory/gameTime.ts:6-15` — existing `GameTimeState` interface with `season` field (Czech names "zima"/"jaro"/"léto"/"podzim"), confirming the codebase's Czech fantasy naming convention
- `src/memory/memoryEngine.ts:266-297` — existing `GAME_TIME_SETTING_PREFIX` and `advanceAndPersistTime` pattern, which the new `CALENDAR_SETTING_PREFIX` and `advanceAndPersistCalendar` mirror
- `src/prompt/promptBuilder.ts:126-129` — existing `gameTimeDescription` optional field in `PromptBuilderInput`, rendered at lines 526-529 as `[PRÁVĚ TEĎ]` block; the new `calendarDateDescription` field and `[DNEŠNÍ DATUM]` rendering follow the same pattern
- `src/ui/chat/ChatScreen.tsx:197` — existing `chat?.title` h1 in the header where the calendar UI was placed alongside
- `src/stores/chatStore.ts:243-257` — existing `buildPrompt` call; verified that `calendarDateDescription` is now passed alongside the other fields
- `git_status` output confirmed 5 modified files and 2 new files (calendar.ts, calendar.test.ts)

### CHANGES

- `src/memory/calendar.ts` — NEW: CalendarDate interface, 12 Czech fantasy months, 4 seasons with effects, pure functions (getSeason, calendarDateFromDays, advanceDay, formatCalendarDate, formatCalendarDateShort, calendarDescription, serialization helpers)
- `src/memory/calendar.test.ts` — NEW: unit tests for all calendar functions, month/season integrity, JSON round-trip, boundary conditions
- `src/db/repositories/settingsRepo.ts` — ADDED: `getCalendarSetting` and `setCalendarSetting` helpers with `CALENDAR_SETTING_KEY = "game_calendar"`
- `src/memory/memoryEngine.ts` — ADDED: calendar imports, `advanceAndPersistCalendar` (advances calendar by 1 day), `ensureCalendarInitialized` (first-write idempotent init)
- `src/prompt/promptBuilder.ts` — ADDED: `calendarDateDescription?: string` field to `PromptBuilderInput`; rendering of `[DNEŠNÍ DATUM]` block in the trailing system message before `[PRÁVĚ TEĎ]`
- `src/stores/chatStore.ts` — ADDED: import of `ensureCalendarInitialized` and `calendarDescription`; calendar loading + description generation in `buildApiMessages`; `calendarDateDescription` passed to `buildPrompt`
- `src/ui/chat/ChatScreen.tsx` — ADDED: import of calendar functions from `settingsRepo` and `calendar.ts`; `calendarDate`/`calendarExpanded` state; useEffect to load calendar per chat; clickable date display in header with popover showing season name, full date, and season effects

### RISKS

- **UI staleness**: The calendar date in ChatScreen loads once on mount and doesn't auto-refresh when `[TIME:+1d]` tag advances it via the prompt processor. Mitigation: a future refresh-on-message-sent hook or a zustand store subscription could keep the UI in sync.
- **Key collision potential**: `memoryEngine.ts` uses `game_calendar_${chatId}` while `settingsRepo.ts` exports `CALENDAR_SETTING_KEY = "game_calendar"` (ChatScreen uses `game_calendar_${chatId}` via `getCalendarSetting`). These are consistent — the `_${chatId}` suffix is appended by the helper. No actual collision, but worth noting the two different patterns exist.
- **No `[TIME:+1d]` tag processing**: The prompt instructs the model to use `[TIME:+1d]` but there is no post-generation tag processor that actually calls `advanceAndPersistCalendar`. The tag instruction is prompt-only for now. Mitigation: add a tag processor in `inventoryProcessor.ts` or similar.

### BLOCKERS

None.

## Task: M20 — Conditions / Buffs / Debuffs System

Add a condition tracking system to personas. Conditions are temporary effects that modify gameplay: "Otrávený (3 dny, -2 Alchymie)", "Požehnaný (1 den, +1 Boj)", "Zlomená noha (7 dní, -50% rychlost)".

### Implementation:

**1. Persona field** — extend the Persona type with `conditions` (already JSON in DB):
```typescript
interface Condition {
  name: string;
  description: string; // what it does
  expiresAt: string | null; // ISO date-time or null = permanent
  modifiers?: { stat: string; value: number }[]; // optional numeric effects
}
```

**2. Already exists:** personas have JSON fields (`skills`, `inventory`) — add `conditions` similarly via migration 13.

**3. AI manages conditions via tags:**
- `[COND:+jméno:popis]` — adds condition
- `[COND:-jméno]` — removes condition
- `[COND:+jméno:popis:7d]` — adds condition expiring in 7 days

**4. Tag parsing** — extend `src/chat/inventoryTags.ts`:
- Parse `[COND:...]` tags
- Export `ConditionMutation` type

**5. Processing** — extend `src/chat/inventoryProcessor.ts`:
- Apply condition mutations to persona

**6. Prompt instruction** — in `src/prompt/promptBuilder.ts`:
- Add condition tag instructions to game tags section
- Only show when persona has progression !== "none"

**7. DB migration (13):**
```sql
ALTER TABLE personas ADD COLUMN conditions TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]';
```

**8. UI** — add conditions display to `PersonaForm.tsx`:
- Read-only list of active conditions
- Show: name, description, expiry (if any)
- Red for negative, green for positive, yellow for neutral

### Key files:
- `src-tauri/src/migrations.rs` — add migration 13
- `src/db/repositories/personasRepo.ts` — add conditions to types, add CRUD helpers
- `src/chat/inventoryTags.ts` — parse `[COND:...]` tags
- `src/chat/inventoryProcessor.ts` — process condition mutations
- `src/prompt/promptBuilder.ts` — condition tag instructions
- `src/ui/personas/PersonaForm.tsx` — show conditions list

## Task: M21 — Faction Reputation System

Add faction reputation tracking. Player can gain/lose reputation with factions, which affects NPC behavior, prices, and quests.

### Implementation:

**1. New DB table + Rust migration (14):**
```sql
CREATE TABLE faction_reputations (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  persona_id TEXT NOT NULL,
  faction_name TEXT NOT NULL,
  reputation INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, -- -100 to +100
  created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
  updated_at TEXT NOT NULL,
  FOREIGN KEY (persona_id) REFERENCES personas(id)
);
```

**2. Repository** — new `src/db/repositories/factionsRepo.ts`:
- `FactionRep` type: { id, personaId, factionName, reputation, createdAt, updatedAt }
- `listFactions(personaId)`, `updateReputation(id, delta)`, `createFaction()`

**3. AI manages factions via tags:**
- `[FACTION:+jméno:10]` — increase reputation (or starts faction at 10)
- `[FACTION:+jméno:-5]` — decrease reputation
- `[FACTION:jméno]` — shows current reputation in prompt

**4. Tag parsing** — extend `src/chat/inventoryTags.ts`:
- Parse `[FACTION:...]` tags
- Export `FactionMutation` type

**5. Processing** — extend `src/chat/inventoryProcessor.ts`:
- Apply faction mutations

**6. Prompt instruction** — in `src/prompt/promptBuilder.ts`:
- Add faction tag instructions to game tags section
- Include current faction standings when persona has any
- Instruction: "NPC reactions should reflect faction reputation. Hostile at <-50, suspicious at <-20, neutral, friendly at >20, allied at >50."

**7. UI** — add factions display to `PersonaForm.tsx`:
- Read-only list of faction standings
- Colored bar: red (-100 to -50), yellow (-49 to -20), gray (-19 to 19), green (20 to 49), gold (50 to 100)

### Key files:
- `src-tauri/src/migrations.rs` — add migration 14
- NEW: `src/db/repositories/factionsRepo.ts`
- `src/chat/inventoryTags.ts` — parse `[FACTION:...]` tags
- `src/chat/inventoryProcessor.ts` — process faction mutations
- `src/prompt/promptBuilder.ts` — faction info in prompt
- `src/ui/personas/PersonaForm.tsx` — show faction list

## Task: M18 — Dice Rolling

Add dice rolling to chat messages. The user types `/roll 2d6+3` in the chat input, the parser extracts the expression, rolls dice, and sends the result as a formatted message.

### Implementation:

**1. Dice parser** — already exists in `src/chat/diceCommand.ts`:
- `isDiceCommand(text)` — returns true if text starts with `/roll`
- `extractDiceExpression(text)` — extracts the dice expression

**2. Rust dice roller** — new file `src-tauri/src/commands/dice.rs` (already exists with `eval_dice` command):
- Already implemented: takes a dice expression string, returns a result object
- Result format: `{ expression, total, rolls: [{ die, results }], modifier }`
- Supported: `2d6`, `1d20+5`, `4d8-2`, `1d100`

**3. ChatInput integration** — already exists in `src/ui/chat/ChatInput.tsx`:
- `isDiceCommand()` check in `submit()`
- `onDiceRoll` callback when dice detected
- Already flashes the input when dice roll

**4. What NEEDS to be added — ChatScreen dice handler:**
- In ChatScreen, the `onDiceRoll` handler should:
  a) Call `invoke("eval_dice", { expression })` to get the roll result
  b) Format a nice message with the dice roll
  c) Insert it as a system message showing dice results
  d) Optionally send it to the AI so it can narrate the outcome

**5. The system message format:**
```
🎲 2d6+3: [4] + [2] + 3 = 9
```
This goes into the chat history so the AI can react to it.

### Key files:
- `src-tauri/src/commands/dice.rs` — already exists, no changes needed
- `src/ui/chat/ChatScreen.tsx` — add dice handler that calls Rust backend and inserts system message
- `src/chat/diceCommand.ts` — already exists
- `src/stores/chatStore.ts` — may need a `sendDiceRoll` or `addSystemMessage` action

### Important:
- The existing `diceCommand.ts` already parses `/roll 2d6+3` format
- The Rust `eval_dice` already handles the math
- Just need to wire them together in ChatScreen
- Use `invoke("eval_dice", { expression })` 
- Insert the formatted result as a system message in the chat

OUTPUT: Summary, what was already there vs what was added.

### SUMMARY
The M18 dice rolling wiring was already implemented in a prior merge (commit `6357cc5` "Merge all 8 worktree branches + fix 11 test failures … dice"). The parser (`diceCommand.ts`), Rust backend (`dice.rs`), ChatInput integration, and ChatScreen `handleDiceRoll` handler are all in place and functional. I fixed one bug: `formatDiceSystemMessage` was duplicating the expression because the Rust `eval_dice` result already includes it — e.g. `[HOD KOSTKOU] 2d6+3 = 2d6+3 = 8 (3+2+3) = 8`. Now it produces `🎲 2d6+3 = 8 (3+2+3) = 8`.

### EVIDENCE
- `src/ui/chat/ChatScreen.tsx:92-110` — `handleDiceRoll` already calls `invoke("eval_dice", { expression })`, formats via `formatDiceSystemMessage`, creates a system message via `createMessage(chatId, "system", content)`, and appends to local state
- `src/ui/chat/ChatScreen.tsx:526` — `onDiceRoll={(expression) => void handleDiceRoll(expression)}` wired to ChatInput
- `src/ui/chat/ChatInput.tsx:67-79` — `isDiceCommand` / `extractDiceExpression` check, `onDiceRoll` call, and dice flash already implemented
- `src/chat/diceCommand.ts:10-12` — `isDiceCommand` uses `/r` prefix (case-insensitive)
- `src/chat/diceCommand.ts:17-19` — `extractDiceExpression` extracts expression after `/r `
- `src-tauri/src/commands/dice.rs:242-288` — `eval_dice` Tauri command returns `Result<String, String>` formatted as `"2d6+3 = 8 (3+2+3) = 8"`
- `src-tauri/src/lib.rs:14,58` — `eval_dice` imported and registered in Tauri builder
- `src/db/repositories/messagesRepo.ts:4` — `MessageRole = "user" | "assistant" | "system"` — "system" is valid
- `src/chat/diceCommand.test.ts` — tests cover `isDiceCommand` and `extractDiceExpression` but not `formatDiceSystemMessage`
- `git log` commit `6357cc5` — "Merge all 8 worktree branches + fix 11 test failures (emotions, gameTime, MMR, chunking, dice)"

### CHANGES
- `src/chat/diceCommand.ts` — fixed `formatDiceSystemMessage` to use 🎲 emoji prefix directly on the Rust result string instead of duplicating the expression with `[HOD KOSTKOU] ${expression} = ${result}`

### RISKS
- The `_expression` parameter (underscore-prefixed unused) relies on TypeScript's convention for suppressing `noUnusedParameters`. The codebase has `noUnusedParameters: true` in `tsconfig.json:20` and no visible precedent for underscore-prefixed unused params in the `src/` tree — if the compiler version doesn't honor the convention, build would fail. Mitigation: remove the parameter and update the single callsite at `ChatScreen.tsx:97` to pass only `result`.
- Dice roll does not auto-trigger an AI response (the task says "optionally send it to the AI"). The system message enters history and the AI sees it on the next user message, but if auto-narration is desired, the handler would need to call `sendMessage` with a follow-up like "The dice have been cast."

### BLOCKERS
None.

## Task: M17 — Quest Journal

Add structured quest tracking with AI-managed progress.

### Design:
- Quests are stored in a new `quests` DB table and managed through tags in AI responses (same pattern as skills/inventory)
- UI: Panel in ChatScreen (same pattern as InventoryPanel — slide-out from the right side, toggle with 📜 button in header)
- Quests have: name, description, status (active/completed/failed), created_at, updated_at
- AI tracks quests via tags: `[QUEST:+jméno]` starts a quest, `[QUEST:✓jméno]` completes, `[QUEST:✗jméno]` fails
- AI can also add progress notes: `[QUEST:jméno:poznámka]`

### Implementation:

**1. DB migration (12):**
```sql
CREATE TABLE quests (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  chat_id TEXT NOT NULL,
  name TEXT NOT NULL,
  description TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
  status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active', -- active, completed, failed
  created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
  updated_at TEXT NOT NULL,
  FOREIGN KEY (chat_id) REFERENCES chats(id)
);
```

**2. Rust migration** (`src-tauri/src/migrations.rs`):
- Add MIGRATION_012 with the above SQL

**3. Repository** (`src/db/repositories/questsRepo.ts`):
- `Quest` type: { id, chatId, name, description, status, createdAt, updatedAt }
- `listQuests(chatId)`, `createQuest()`, `updateQuestStatus()`, `addQuestNote()`

**4. Tag parsing** — extend `src/chat/inventoryTags.ts`:
- Add quest tag parsing: `[QUEST:+name]`, `[QUEST:✓name]`, `[QUEST:✗name]`, `[QUEST:name:note]`
- Export `QuestMutation` type

**5. Processing** — extend `src/chat/inventoryProcessor.ts`:
- Process quest mutations alongside inventory/skill mutations
- Create/update quests in DB

**6. Prompt instruction** — in `src/prompt/promptBuilder.ts`:
- Add quest tag instructions to the game tags section
- Only show when persona has progression !== "none"

**7. UI Panel** — new `src/ui/chat/QuestPanel.tsx`:
- Slide-out panel like InventoryPanel
- List quests grouped by status (active first, then completed, then failed)
- Each quest shows: name, description, status badge
- 📜 toggle button in ChatScreen header

**8. ChatScreen integration**:
- Add `questsOpen` state
- Add 📜 button next to 🎒 (Inventory) and 🧠 (Memory) buttons
- Load quests when panel opens

- NEW: `src/db/repositories/questsRepo.ts`
- NEW: `src/ui/chat/QuestPanel.tsx`
- MODIFY: `src-tauri/src/migrations.rs`
- MODIFY: `src/chat/inventoryTags.ts`
- MODIFY: `src/chat/inventoryProcessor.ts`
- MODIFY: `src/prompt/promptBuilder.ts`
- MODIFY: `src/ui/chat/ChatScreen.tsx`

Added a `quests` DB table (migration 012), a TypeScript repository (`questsRepo.ts`), tag parsing for `[QUEST:…]` syntax in `inventoryTags.ts`, DB-aware processing in `inventoryProcessor.ts`, prompt instruction emission in `promptBuilder.ts`, a slide-out UI panel (`QuestPanel.tsx`), header button integration in `ChatScreen.tsx`, and i18n keys for Czech and English. The implementation follows the same pattern as the existing inventory/skill system.

### EVIDENCE
- `src-tauri/src/migrations.rs:74-79` — MIGRATION_012 registered in `all_migrations()`
- `src-tauri/src/migrations.rs:321-335` — MIGRATION_012 SQL with quests table + index

- `src/i18n/en.json:95-101` — English quests i18n keys (valid JSON confirmed)

### CHANGES
- `src-tauri/src/migrations.rs` — Added MIGRATION_012 (quests table + index)
- `src/db/repositories/questsRepo.ts` — NEW: Quest type, listQuests, createQuest, updateQuestStatus, addQuestNote, getQuestByName
- `src/chat/inventoryTags.ts` — Added QuestMutation interface, quest tag parsing (start/complete/fail/note), updated ParsedTags + return

- The `QuestPanel` renders inside the same flex row as chat content, competing for horizontal space with MemoryPanel and InventoryPanel — if both panels are open simultaneously, the chat area may become very narrow. This matches the existing pattern but could be improved with tabbed panels later.

None.

## Task: Chat Message Search

Add full-text search across all chat messages.

### Requirements:
1. **Search input** at the top of the chat message list (or in the sidebar, or as a collapsible bar)
2. **SQLite FTS5** for fast full-text search — use `SELECT * FROM messages WHERE content LIKE ?` for simplicity (FTS5 is overkill for single-user desktop)
3. **Debounced**: 300ms debounce to avoid reloading on every keystroke
4. **Results**: Show matches as a list below the search bar, with:
   - Snippet of the matching text (first 100 chars)
   - Timestamp of the message
   - Click to scroll to that message in the chat
5. **Scope**: Search in current chat only (not across all chats — keep it simple)
6. **UI placement**: Add a 🔍 icon in the ChatScreen header that toggles the search bar
7. **Keyboard shortcut**: Ctrl+F opens search (when chat is focused)

### Key files:
- `src/ui/chat/ChatScreen.tsx` — add search toggle and search bar
- `src/db/repositories/messagesRepo.ts` — add `searchMessages(chatId: string, query: string)` function with LIKE query
- `src/ui/chat/MessageList.tsx` — support `scrollToMessageId` prop

### Important:
- Use `useState` + `useEffect` with a setTimeout debounce for the search
- `SELECT id, content, created_at FROM messages WHERE chat_id = $1 AND content LIKE $2 ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 20`
- LIKE pattern: `%query%` (case-insensitive via SQLite's default behavior)
- Clicking a result → set `scrollToMessageId` on MessageList, which scrolls to that message and highlights it
- Simple highlight: when `scrollToMessageId` matches a message id, add a CSS class with a yellow background

OUTPUT: Summary, files changed.

## Task: Quick Action Buttons for Chat

Add customizable quick action buttons above the chat input that insert predefined text.

### Requirements:
1. **Add a row of small buttons** above the ChatInput textarea (before the suggestions row)
2. Each button inserts a predefined text into the input when clicked

## Task: M15 — Progression system (skill-based / level-based / none)

The app currently supports only skill-based progression. Add a `progression_type` field to personas to switch between systems.

### Already exists:
- Personas have `skills` (JSON array of `{name, level}`) and `inventory`
- Game tags `[SKILL:...]` and `[INV:...]` are parsed from AI responses
- `promptBuilder.ts` generates game tag instructions for the AI

### What to implement:

**1. Add `progression` field to Persona** (DB + types):
- `src-tauri/src/migrations.rs` — migration 11: `ALTER TABLE personas ADD COLUMN progression TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'skill'`
- `src/db/repositories/personasRepo.ts` — add `progression: "skill" | "level" | "none"` to Persona interface
- Default for new personas: `"skill"`

**2. Update PersonaForm:**
- Add a radio/select for progression type
- Options: "Dovednosti (skill-based)" / "Úrovně (level-based)" / "Bez progrese"
- i18n keys in cs.json and en.json

**3. Update promptBuilder.ts:**
- Only emit [SKILL:...] tag instructions when `progression === "skill"`
- Only emit inventory tag instructions when inventory exists (regardless of progression)
- For `"none"`: no game tag instructions at all
- For `"level"`: add `[LEVEL:...]` tag instruction (format: `[LEVEL:xp_gained]` or `[LEVEL:+level]`)

**4. Update inventoryProcessor.ts:**
- Rename `processInventoryResponse` → `processGameResponse` (it already handles both)
- Add level tag parsing: `[LEVEL:+xp]` adds XP, `[LEVEL:name+xp]` adds to a specific skill/stat
- Store XP/level on persona (add optional `xp?: number`, `level?: number` to Persona)

**5. Keep it simple:**
- `"skill"` mode: current behavior (tags + skill DB updates)
- `"level"` mode: store XP/level on persona, `[LEVEL:+n]` tags
- `"none"` mode: no tags, no processing

### Key files:
- `src-tauri/src/migrations.rs`
- `src/db/repositories/personasRepo.ts`
- `src/ui/personas/PersonaForm.tsx`
- `src/prompt/promptBuilder.ts`
- `src/chat/inventoryProcessor.ts`
- `src/i18n/cs.json`, `src/i18n/en.json`

OUTPUT: Summary, what progression types are supported, files changed.

## Task: M14 — Auto-illustration background queue

The app has image generation working (`generate_illustration` Tauri command via Gemini/Pollinations). Now add a background queue that auto-generates images for locked facts and inventory items.

### Already exists:
- `generate_illustration` Rust command — takes ConnectionDto + prompt, returns saved PNG path
- `ledger_facts.image_path TEXT` column (migration 8)
- `InventoryEntry` has optional `image_path?: string`
- `image_gen_enabled` setting (0/1), `image_gen_limit` (daily limit, 0=off), `image_gen_connection_id`
- `invoke("generate_illustration", { connection, prompt })` available from frontend

### What to implement:

**1. `src/memory/imageGenQueue.ts`** (new file):
```typescript
// In-memory FIFO queue — one image generated at a time
// On each tick: if queue not empty && enabled, pop next, call invoke, update DB
// Check image_gen_enabled setting before processing
// Use image_gen_connection_id to find connection; fall back to first connection with purposes.includes("image")
// On success: update the target's image_path
// On failure: log and skip
export function enqueueIllustration(type: "fact" | "inventory", targetId: string, prompt: string): void
```

**2. Trigger points:**
- In `ledgerRepo.ts` `setFactLocked()` — after locking a fact, enqueue if no image exists
- In `personasRepo.ts` `updatePersonaInventory()` — after adding an inventory item, enqueue if no image exists

**3. The prompt for image generation** should be descriptive:
- Facts: `"Fantasy illustration: {fact_content}"`
- Inventory: `"Fantasy RPG item: {item_name}"`

**4. Rate limiting:** Check `image_gen_enabled` setting. If `image_gen_limit > 0`, track daily count in memory (reset on date change). Skip if limit reached.

### Key files:
- New: `src/memory/imageGenQueue.ts`
- Modify: `src/db/repositories/ledgerRepo.ts` — add trigger in setFactLocked
- Modify: `src/db/repositories/personasRepo.ts` — add trigger in updatePersonaInventory
- Modify: `src/memory/memoryEngine.ts` — start queue processing on app init

### Important:
- Use `invoke("generate_illustration", ...)` directly (the Tauri IPC bridge)
- Use `getSetting()` for reading settings
- The queue processes one at a time (no parallelism — respect API rate limits)
- Errors should be logged to console, not thrown
- Target IDs for facts: use `fact.id`; for inventory: use `persona.id` (all items belong to a persona)

OUTPUT: Summary of changes, files created/modified, any issues.

workers.result_summary:
All changes are complete and consistent. Here's the summary.

### SUMMARY
Implemented the M14 auto-illustration background queue — a new `src/memory/imageGenQueue.ts` module that maintains an in-memory FIFO queue, processes one image at a time via `invoke("generate_illustration", ...)`, checks `image_gen_enabled`/`image_gen_limit` settings with daily rate limiting, resolves the image-generation connection from `image_gen_connection_id` (falling back to the first connection with `"image"` purpose), and persists results to `ledger_facts.image_path` or `InventoryEntry.image_path`. Trigger points were added in `setFactLocked` (ledgerRepo) and `updatePersona`/`updatePersonaInventory` (personasRepo), plus a side-effect import in `memoryEngine.ts` to load the queue on app init. Circular dependencies are avoided via dynamic `import()` calls.

### EVIDENCE
- `src/memory/imageGenQueue.ts:1-169` — new file, full queue logic
- `src/db/repositories/ledgerRepo.ts:6-10` — `LedgerFact` gains `imagePath: string | null`
- `src/db/repositories/ledgerRepo.ts:22` — `LedgerFactRow` gains `image_path: string | null`
- `src/db/repositories/ledgerRepo.ts:37` — `toFact` maps `imagePath: row.image_path`
- `src/db/repositories/ledgerRepo.ts:90` — `createFact` returns `imagePath: null`
- `src/db/repositories/ledgerRepo.ts:112-118` — new `getFact(id)` function
- `src/db/repositories/ledgerRepo.ts:120-125` — new `setFactImage(id, imagePath)` function
- `src/db/repositories/ledgerRepo.ts:134-141` — `setFactLocked` enqueues on lock if no image
- `src/db/repositories/personasRepo.ts:165-193` — `updatePersona` detects new inventory items and enqueues
- `src/db/repositories/personasRepo.ts:197-208` — new `updatePersonaInventory` function
- `src/db/repositories/personasRepo.ts:214-232` — new `setInventoryItemImage` function
- `src/memory/memoryEngine.ts:36` — side-effect `import "./imageGenQueue"`
- `git_status` at workspace root shows: M ledgerRepo.ts, M personasRepo.ts, M memoryEngine.ts, ?? imageGenQueue.ts

### CHANGES
- Created `src/memory/imageGenQueue.ts` — in-memory FIFO queue with rate limiting, connection resolution, and DB persistence
- Edited `src/db/repositories/ledgerRepo.ts` — added `imagePath` to `LedgerFact`/`LedgerFactRow`, added `getFact`/`setFactImage` helpers, hooked auto-illustration into `setFactLocked`
- Edited `src/db/repositories/personasRepo.ts` — added `updatePersonaInventory` and `setInventoryItemImage`, hooked auto-illustration into `updatePersona`
- Edited `src/memory/memoryEngine.ts` — added side-effect import of `./imageGenQueue` for app-init loading

### RISKS
- **Race condition on concurrent queue access**: `processing` flag and `queue` array are plain module-level variables with no synchronization — safe in a single-threaded browser JS context, but could misbehave if `scheduleProcess` fires while `processQueue` is still resolving an async operation. Mitigation: the `processing` flag gates the loop, and `scheduleProcess` only sets a single timer; no new timer is created while one is pending.
- **`updatePersonaInventory` is a separate function not called by existing UI**: the trigger in `updatePersona` covers the primary update path, but `updatePersonaInventory` itself must be called explicitly from UI code for inventory-only updates. Mitigation: call `updatePersonaInventory` from the personas store or UI where inventory-only edits are made.
- **Inventory item matching by name is case-insensitive but not collision-proof**: two items with the same name (different case) would be treated as the same. Mitigation: enforce unique item names at the UI level.

### BLOCKERS
None.
